title: Determination of the time-variable geopotential by means of orbiting clocks
justification: The GRACE gravity field models employed as the true and nominal gravity fields and the gravity field solutions generated in this study are available from the corresponding author upon request

title: Testing global geopotential models through comparison of a local quasi-geoid model with GPS/leveling data
justification: The satellite missions CHAMP and GRACE provide accurate data that are routinely inverted into spherical harmonic coefficients of the geopotential forming a global geopotential model (GGM). ... This article discusses an alternative validation procedure based on comparison of the GGMs with independent data represented by a set of GPS/leveling stations.

title: Monitoring GOCE gradiometer calibration parameters using accelerometer and star sensor data: methodology and first results
justification: The results are validated by investigating the trace of the calibrated gravity gradients and comparing calibrated gravity gradients with reference gradients computed along the GOCE orbit using the ITG-Grace-2010s gravity field model.

title: Detectability of Seamount Eruptions Through a Quantum Technology Gravity Mission MOCAST+: Hunga Tonga, Fani Maoré and Other Smaller Eruptions
venue: Fani Maoré and Other Smaller Eruptions
justification: We review documented eruptions to define whether they could be detected by a future satellite gravity mission. ... We review further documented submarine eruptions and estimate the upper limit of the expected gravity changes.
